title: GitHub Integration Hardening — 5 surgical fixes with critic conditions
boundary: GitHub Integration Hardening — revive GitHubService, error UI, account delete cascade, IPC extraction + PAT validation, safe error casts
consumer_purpose: Harden the already-implemented GitHub integration in the desktop app: wire dead service into runtime, show auth error in review-tab, cascade token cleanup on account delete, extract GitHub IPC from god module, validate PAT format, fix fragile proxy error serialization, and eliminate unsafe error casts
claim_atoms: GitHubService.layer is wired into AppLayer and callable from any Effect in the runtime
review-tab.tsx shows github.noAuth message when GitHub token is missing instead of silently disabling the button
Account delete flow calls github-clear-token IPC handler to clean up stored GitHub token

GitHub IPC handlers (github-oauth-start, github-oauth-callback, github-get-token, github-set-token, github-clear-token, github-api-proxy) are extracted from ipc.ts into packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts with a registerGithubIpcHandlers() registration function
github-set-token validates PAT format (ghp_*, github_pat_*, ghu_* prefix) before storing
github-api-proxy error serialization uses structured return {error: {type, hostname, allowedHostnames}} instead of throw new Error(JSON.stringify(...))
All 4 unsafe error casts (error as Error) in service.ts Effect.tryPromise catch callbacks are replaced with proper type narrowing

Scope refocused to hardening only (5 narrow fixes) vs original broad 5-fix OAuth+PR plan.", "root_causes": [ { "id": "rc-a", "hypothesis": "GitHubService.layer exists in packages/opencode/src/github/service.ts but is never imported into AppLayer in app-runtime.ts. The service is dead code — zero runtime registrations.", "confidence": "high", "evidence": [ "packages/opencode/src/github/service.ts exports `layer` function but it's not imported in packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts", "No references to 'github' in app-runtime.ts imports list (lines 1-61)" ] }, { "id": "rc-b", "hypothesis": "github.noAuth i18n key (en.ts:593) exists but is never referenced in review-tab.tsx. The Create PR button is silently disabled when no token is present with no user feedback.", "confidence": "high", "evidence": [ "en.ts line 593: 'github.noAuth': 'Connect GitHub to create PRs' — defined but unreferenced", "review-tab.tsx line 146: `hasToken()` returns !!token(), used only for Button disabled prop", "No github.noAuth identifier in review-tab.tsx" ] }, { "id": "rc-c", "hypothesis": "Account deletion in the desktop app does not cascade to clear the GitHub token stored in electron-store. github-clear-token IPC handler exists but is never called from the account delete flow.", "confidence": "medium", "evidence": [ "github-clear-token IPC handler exists at ipc.ts:461", "No greppable references to githubClearToken or github-clear-token in any settings/account UI file", "out-of-scope-findings.jsonl records this as cross-process cleanup debt" ] }, { "id": "rc-d", "hypothesis": "ipc.ts at 508 lines is a god module hosting PKCE OAuth, safeStorage, and API proxy next to MCP, agents, plugins, and OS handlers. The desktop-menu-actions.ts extraction pattern exists as a precedent but GitHub handlers were never extracted.", "confidence": "high", "evidence": [ "ipc.ts: 508 lines, 6 GitHub-specific handlers (github-oauth-start, github-oauth-callback, github-get-token, github-set-token, github-clear-token, github-api-proxy)", "desktop-menu-actions.ts: 85-line separate module with runDesktopMenuAction export — proven extraction pattern", "Critic finding F1 filed as BLOCKING severity" ] }, { "id": "rc-e", "hypothesis": "githubSetToken IPC handler accepts any string without format validation. GitHub PATs follow well-known prefix patterns (ghp_*, github_pat_*, ghu_*) that can be validated at the IPC boundary.", "confidence": "high", "evidence": [ "ipc.ts line 455-458: github-set-token handler only checks typeof string + truthiness", "No prefix validation against known GitHub PAT formats", "Critic finding F2 filed — surface area concern" ] }, { "id": "rc-f", "hypothesis": "github-api-proxy error serialization uses throw new Error(JSON.stringify(...)) which buries structured fields inside Error.message. IPC serialization loses the structure — renderer must JSON.parse error.message to recover fields.", "confidence": "high", "evidence": [ "ipc.ts line 468-470: throw new Error(JSON.stringify({ type: 'GitHubProxyError', ... }))", "ipc.ts line 473-475: same pattern for hostname rejection", "Critic finding F3 filed" ] }, { "id": "rc-g", "hypothesis": "service.ts Effect.tryPromise catch callbacks use `error as Error` which is an unsafe type assertion. The caught error may not be Error-type, silently widening to `any` in Effect's error channel.", "confidence": "high", "evidence": [ "service.ts line 87: catch: (error) => error as Error", "service.ts line 120: catch: (error) => error as Error", "service.ts line 148: catch: (error) => error as Error", "service.ts line 183: catch: (error) => error as Error", "These are in Effect.tryPromise catch handlers where handleResponse might throw GitHubApiError but network errors throw arbitrary types" ] } ], "fixes": [ { "id": "fix-A", "description": "Wire GitHubService.layer into AppLayer in app-runtime.ts — add import and Layer.mergeAll entry. Add smoke test that verifies the layer composes without error.", "files": [ "packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts", "packages/opencode/src/github/service.ts" ], "before": "exports const layer = (token: string) => Layer... — token required at layer construction time. Dynamic token means it can't be a static Layer.mergeAll entry.", "after": "Add a default layer to GitHubService that wraps the layer factory with token lookup from config/env. Or add a no-op placeholder layer that creates a default service reading token from global config. Import in app-runtime.ts and add to Layer.mergeAll.", "impact": { "affected_files": [ "packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts", "packages/opencode/src/github/service.ts" ], "tests_to_run": [ "bun typecheck in packages/opencode", "bun test --filter 'github' in packages/opencode" ] }, "risks": [ "GitHubService.layer requires a token string at construction — can't simply add to Layer.mergeAll without a default/placeholder", "Solution: add a GitHubServiceLayer that reads token from config service or environment, then calls layer() internally", "If no token is available, the layer should still compose but all operations will fail with auth errors — acceptable v1 behavior" ], "validation": [ "bun typecheck in packages/opencode — AppLayer type must still compose", "Smoke test: AppRuntime.runPromise(GitHubService) does not throw during layer composition" ] }, { "id": "fix-B", "description": "Add github.noAuth error message to review-tab.tsx — when hasToken() is false, show the i18n string next to or in place of the disabled Create PR button.", "files": [ "packages/app/src/pages/session/review-tab.tsx" ], "before": "Button is disabled when !hasToken() with no explanation. User sees a grayed button and doesn't know why.", "after": "When !hasToken(), render a {language.t('github.noAuth')} label below or instead of the Button. Or: keep Button but show tooltip/badge explaining why it's disabled.", "impact": { "affected_files": [ "packages/app/src/pages/session/review-tab.tsx" ], "tests_to_run": [ "bun typecheck in packages/app" ] }, "risks": [ "SolidJS conditional rendering: the message should appear when no token but disappear seamlessly when token loads asynchronously", "useLanguage context must be available — already imported at line 14", "i18n key github.noAuth already exists in en.ts — no new i18n file changes needed" ], "validation": [ "bun typecheck in packages/app", "Manual: open review-tab without GitHub auth — verify 'Connect GitHub to create PRs' message is visible" ] }, { "id": "fix-C", "description": "Wire github-clear-token into the account delete flow. Identify the account deletion code path and add a call to the existing githubClearToken (or github-clear-token IPC) before or after the account is deleted.", "files": [ "packages/app/src/pages/settings/account-settings.tsx (or equivalent account delete UI)" ], "before": "git push origin main", "after": "git push origin main", "impact": { "affected_files": [ "packages/app/src/pages/settings/account-settings.tsx" ], "tests_to_run": [ "bun typecheck in packages/app" ] }, "risks": [ "Account delete flow needs investigation — exact file may differ across codebase versions", "githubClearToken must be accessible from the account delete code path (might need IPC invoke)", "Token cleanup is local-only (electron-store delete) — server-side token revocation is deferred (follow-up issue from critic)" ], "validation": [ "bun typecheck in packages/app", "Trace account delete flow manually: confirm githubClearToken is invoked on delete" ] }, { "id": "fix-D", "description": "Three critic-mandated changes: (D1) Extract GitHub IPC handlers into github-ipc.ts, (D2) Add PAT format validation to githubSetToken, (D3) Fix API proxy error serialization.", "files": [ "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts", "packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts (new)" ], "before": "ipc.ts: 508 lines, 6 GitHub handlers inline, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/GITHUB_TOKEN_URL constants, PkceState type, generateCodeVerifier/generateCodeChallenge helpers, pendingOAuth Map, encryptToken/decryptToken, ALLOWED_GITHUB_HOSTNAMES. github-set-token only checks typeof string + truthiness. github-api-proxy throws Error(JSON.stringify(...)).", "after": "D1: Create packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts with export function registerGithubIpcHandlers(): register 6 GitHub handlers, move GITHUB_CLIENT_ID/GITHUB_TOKEN_URL constants, PkceState type, generateCodeVerifier/generateCodeChallenge, pendingOAuth Map, encryptToken/decryptToken, ALLOWED_GITHUB_HOSTNAMES into the module. ipc.ts imports and calls registerGithubIpcHandlers() inside registerIpcHandlers(). D2: In github-set-token, add PAT prefix validation: /^(ghp_|github_pat_|ghu_|gho_|ghb_)/.test(token). Throw descriptive error if format doesn't match. D3: Replace throw new Error(JSON.stringify({...GitHubProxyError...})) with a structured return: return {error: {type: 'GitHubProxyError', hostname: parsed.hostname, allowedHostnames: ALLOWED_GITHUB_HOSTNAMES, message: 'Hostname not allowed'}}.", "impact": { "affected_files": [ "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts", "packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts (new)" ], "tests_to_run": [ "bun typecheck in packages/desktop" ] }, "risks": [ "D1: registerGithubIpcHandlers() signature must match what ipc.ts expects — should be a void function with no deps parameter (GitHub handlers don't need Deps), called after Deps-based registrations complete", "D1: encryptToken/decryptToken use safeStorage which is already imported in ipc.ts — github-ipc.ts will need to import safeStorage independently", "D1: pendingOAuth Map is module-scoped — moving to new module changes nothing functionally but diffs will show large code motion", "D2: PAT prefix validation should be documented inline — GitHub PAT formats may evolve (new prefixes), use regex that covers current known patterns: ghp_ (classic), github_pat_ (fine-grained), ghu_ (GitHub CLI), gho_ (GitHub Actions), ghb_ (GitHub App install)", "D2: Validation failure should return a clear IPC error message: 'GitHub PAT must start with ghp_, github_pat_, ghu_, gho_, or ghb_'", "D3: The structured error return changes the IPC contract — any consumer expecting throw must now handle {error: ...} in the return value. Update all callers of github-api-proxy (currently only in service.ts proxy mode).", "D3: Return value must clearly distinguish success vs error without type confusion: return {status: number, body: string} on success OR {error: {...}} on error.", "D3: The 'Invalid URL' case with null hostname should still return {error: {type: 'GitHubProxyError', hostname: null, ...}}" ], "validation": [ "bun typecheck in packages/desktop", "Verify registerGithubIpcHandlers() is called inside registerIpcHandlers()", "Verify github-set-token rejects 'invalid-token-no-prefix' with descriptive error", "Verify github-set-token accepts 'ghp_validFormatToken'", "Verify github-set-token accepts 'github_pat_validFormatToken'", "Verify github-api-proxy returns {error: {type: 'GitHubProxyError', hostname, allowedHostnames}} for non-allowlist URLs instead of throwing", "Verify github-api-proxy returns {status, body} for valid requests" ] }, { "id": "fix-E", "description": "Replace unsafe `error as Error` casts in service.ts Effect.tryPromise catch callbacks with proper type narrowing that handles both GitHubApiError (from handleResponse) and unknown errors.", "files": [ "packages/opencode/src/github/service.ts" ], "before": "4 instances of: catch: (error) => error as Error — raw type assertion that lies when error isn't a GitHub error type", "after": "Replace each with: catch: (error) => error instanceof GitHubApiError ? error : new GitHubApiError({message: String(error), status: 0}) — handles both expected GitHubApiError and unexpected errors safely.", "impact": { "affected_files": [ "packages/opencode/src/github/service.ts" ], "tests_to_run": [ "bun typecheck in packages/opencode", "bun test in packages/opencode (existing GitHub tests)" ] }, "risks": [ "The catch callback return type must match the method's Error type (GitHubApiError | GitHubAuthError). Since handleResponse throws GitHubApiError, and GitHubAuthError is used for auth failures (handled separately), casting to GitHubApiError covers all cases.", "If error is already GitHubApiError, pass it through directly (preserves message and status).", "If error is anything else (TypeError, SyntaxError, etc.), wrap in GitHubApiError with status 0 — this is safe and debug-friendly.", "The Effect error channel type guarantees consuming code handles Error = GitHubApiError | GitHubAuthError." ], "validation": [ "bun typecheck in packages/opencode", "bun test in packages/opencode — verify existing GitHub tests still pass with corrected error types", "Manual: verify GitHubApiError.status is preserved when handleResponse throws it (should be non-zero) vs unknown errors get status 0" ] } ], "apply_order": ["fix-D", "fix-E", "fix-A", "fix-B", "fix-C"], "dependencies": { "fix-D": [], "fix-E": [], "fix-A": ["fix-D"], "fix-B": [], "fix-C": ["fix-D"] }, "risks": [ "Shared file contention: packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts is modified by multiple concurrent lanes. fix-D extracts GitHub handlers — other lanes may have added handlers after the GitHub block. Must preserve all existing registrations.", "Shared file contention: packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts has unstaged changes (Layer type assertion). fix-A edit must be additive — just add import and Layer.mergeAll entry, do not touch existing code.", "Shared file contention: packages/app/src/pages/session/review-tab.tsx has edit history from multiple lanes. fix-B edit must be additive — add conditional rendering block, do not modify existing event handlers or scroll logic.", "PAT format validation is a string prefix check — not cryptographically verified. A string starting with ghp_ but not actually a valid GitHub PAT will pass validation. This is acceptable — validation is a UX aid, not a security guarantee.", "github-api-proxy structured return changes IPC contract. If any caller (outside this lane) expects throw, they'll break silently. Search for all callers of electronAPI.githubApiProxy / 'github-api-proxy' before applying.", "Account delete flow file path may differ from expected (account-settings.tsx). If the file doesn't exist or has different structure, fix-C may need path adjustment.", "GitHubService default layer composition may introduce import cycle if it depends on Config which is already in Layer.mergeAll. Use dynamic Effect to read config inside the layer, not at module level." ], "rollback_plan": "Revert in reverse order: fix-C (remove githubClearToken call from account delete), fix-B (remove conditional rendering block in review-tab), fix-A (remove GitHubService import and layer entry from app-runtime), fix-E (revert error cast changes in service.ts), fix-D (revert in 3 parts: D3 revert structured return to throw pattern + D2 remove PAT validation + D1 inline github-ipc.ts back into ipc.ts)", "validation_strategy": { "step_1": "bun typecheck in packages/desktop — validates fix-D (new github-ipc.ts, modified ipc.ts)", "step_2": "bun typecheck in packages/opencode — validates fix-A and fix-E", "step_3": "bun typecheck in packages/app — validates fix-B and fix-C", "step_4": "bun test --filter 'github' in packages/opencode — validates fix-E error type correctness", "step_5": "Manual: verify github-set-token rejects bad prefix, accepts valid prefix", "step_6": "Manual: verify github-api-proxy returns structured error object instead of throwing", "step_7": "Manual: open review-tab without token — verify 'Connect GitHub to create PRs' message appears" }, "shared_file_coordination": { "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts": "fix-D extracts ~120 lines (GitHub handlers + helpers + constants) into github-ipc.ts. Import registerGithubIpcHandlers and call it inside registerIpcHandlers(). Must preserve pre-existing handlers from other lanes.", "packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts": "fix-A adds one import line + one line in Layer.mergeAll. File has unstaged changes (Layer type assertion) — edit must be additive only.", "packages/app/src/pages/session/review-tab.tsx": "fix-B adds conditional rendering block. File has edit history from multiple lanes — edit must be additive only, preserving scroll/restore logic." }, "new_files": [ "packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts — registerGithubIpcHandlers() function, GitHub OAuth/DAL/PKCE/API proxy handlers moved from ipc.ts" ], "modified_files": [ "packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts — extract GitHub handlers, add import + call of registerGithubIpcHandlers()", "packages/opencode/src/github/service.ts — fix 4 unsafe error casts", "packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts — add GitHubService layer", "packages/app/src/pages/session/review-tab.tsx — add github.noAuth error UI", "packages/app/src/pages/settings/account-settings.tsx — add githubClearToken call on account delete" ] } }
